codeReview.summary: Implementation covers the feature description and fulfills VAL-ROLES-008/009/010. The new role instructions enforce web search before hypothesis creation, define KB taxonomy labeling under sc-research-kb, require hypothesis provenance metadata with the four required fields, and include explicit stateless context reconstruction commands. I found one non-blocking reliability issue in settings hook PATH configuration.

issues.description: Hook commands prepend $HOME/go/bin and $HOME/bin, but this environment exposes gt/bd in /home/ubuntu/gopath/bin. In shells where gopath/bin is absent from PATH, SessionStart/PreCompact/UserPromptSubmit/Stop hook commands can fail to find gt. See also lines 23, 32, 43, 54, 65, 76 and environment guidance in .factory/library/environment.md lines 18 and 23.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The integration-worker procedure does not explicitly state that Gas Town role files should be both deployed to ~/gt/scbench/polecats/<role>/.claude/ and mirrored into research/roles/<role>/ for versioned persistence. Worker had to infer this by inspecting prior commits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session f1b80f1b-fffb-4f74-b6c8-902ec0b08657 includes: "The files are in ~/gt/scbench/polecats/idea-factory/, which is outside the git repo..." followed by "I see! The previous roles were committed to `research/roles/` ... AND separately deployed ..."

sharedStateObservations.observation: Role settings templates appear to use a non-canonical PATH for gt hooks. Shared guidance should define one canonical hook PATH to avoid repeated drift across role settings.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: research/roles/idea-factory/settings.json lines 14/23/32/43/54/65/76 use $HOME/go/bin and $HOME/bin; .factory/library/environment.md lines 18 and 23 document gt binaries in /home/ubuntu/gopath/bin and require adding that path.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit 154b344. Feature requirements and mapped contract assertions are met; review passes with one non-blocking settings PATH reliability issue and two shared-state observations.
